title: Genesis: Providence and Blessing
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of genesis: providence and blessing and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study genesis: providence and blessing and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What final request did Jacob make before his death regarding his burial? Answer: Jacob asked to be buried with his fathers in the cave of Machpelah in Canaan, not in Egypt.
segments.correct_answer: to be buried in the cave of Machpelah in Canaan with his fathers
acceptable_variants: bury me in the cave in canaan not egypt
to be carried back to the promised land for burial
buried with Abraham and Isaac in Machpelah

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The cave Abraham purchased as a burial site; Abraham, Sarah, Isaac, Rebekah, Leah, and Jacob were buried there, marking the patriarchs' claim on the promised land
Jacob's final words to his twelve sons in Genesis 49, including the messianic promise to Judah that the scepter would not depart from his line
